BOISE, May Ex-senator w.
i. Borah has got omre to think
about than anybody. He has a large
law practice, is special counsel for
the state in the Haywood case,
counsel for the defendants in some
land fraud cases set for trial in the
northern part of the state this
month, and, 1t has been reported, a
grand jury has voted an indictment
against him on account of some al
legged government land irregulari-
ties.


No official announcement of such
an indictment has been made, but
>Borah, the United States district at
torney and the federal judge have
recently visited the president for
the discussion Of that subject.


Charges were made at the White
House that the district attorney did
not like Borah and nd promoted in
dictment proceedings against him
as a favor to the Western Fedora
ton of Miners, of which w. ID. Hay
wood is secretary. That charge has
not taken root hereabouts and prob
ably not at Washington.


Withal he's not prey to worry.
A call at his office disclosed him in
a room lined from floor to ceiling
with law books, shaping up authori-
ties for the Haywood case. When |
asked him as to the status of the
trial he said:


1T is not possible to forecast
with any certainty the length of the
trial, but In the absence of the I'M
expected | should consider 1t a fair
guess that the end will be reached
in the first week of July.


you are going to hear wonder
full story when the jury is finally Sc
cured" continued the senator. II
looked it over again last night, and
1t is beyond anything | ever read.
The matters involved are suggestive
of the work of the Molly Maguires,
but their methods, like other things
In the world, have been subject to
evolution, and here we have their
latest development. have never
said that the men under arrest COM
mitted these offenses; that is mat
ter for the jury to determine. All
that ! say Is that somebody did
commit them. and that the dis
closure will startle the entire coun-
try


With regard to the charges
against him Borah has said that he
would be ready to meet any indict-
ment against him 30 minutes after
1t was reported. He will probably
try to secure postponement of the
land fraud cases in northern Idaho
If that is not done his clients will
have to get other counsel. With
him the big issue is the Haywood
case.
